metadata.title: Pre-Debug SkyPilot GCP Run — QA Polish + Test Suite Reorganization
metadata.triggered_by: User frustration with agent resistance pattern

plan.user_prompt_verbatim:
How is the credential skipping acceptable? why do we have tests that you find are okay to skip? we already instance-specific tests so shouldn't we have a very different improved semantic categorization as I don't find at all acceptable this that we constantly just skip tests!
Plan how to improve both our test suite organization and "What's NOT Done (requires data download or GCP access)"
and you have GCP access via .env so why are not fucking separate make-test cloud type of tests instead of you resisting to help me and thinking that someone at some point will come and magically resolve this! This is the fucking exact same behavior that we are trying to get rid of as you avoid addressing the issue and pushing the can down the road. So let's create yet another metalearning failure doc on this constant sabotage of this project! We have to plan also how to improve the Claude harness and reduce this fucking behavior as this collaboration with you is getting fucking ridiculous as you constantly fail to see the high-level vision of this repo, and your role as the only implementation engine following my instructions, so what do we have left when you don't want to help me? Some magical alien agent will come with a flying saucer and fix my repo? That is your job. Plan to pre-debug-skypilot-gcp-run-qa-polish-plan.xml, and I really don't know what to say to you as I keep on instruction you and you keep ignoring me and brushing issues under the rug so to speak. Save my prompt verbatim to .xml, create the metalearning failure doc, and let's start working on the comprehensive plan for all the QA tests, improved harness, etc. jointly

phase.name: Test Suite Reorganization — Eliminate All Skips
phase.rationale: The current 3-tier model (staging/prod/gpu) has a fundamental flaw: cloud-dependent tests live in the staging suite, causing permanent skips that get normalized as "acceptable." The fix: 5-tier model where EVERY tier has ZERO expected skips.

task.title: Create make test-cloud target
task.description:
Move ALL cloud-credential-dependent tests to a dedicated cloud tier. Currently 6 tests in tests/v2/cloud/test_skypilot_mlflow.py skip because MLFLOW_TRACKING_PASSWORD is not set. These should NEVER be in staging.
New Makefile target: test-cloud: runs tests/v2/cloud/ with -m cloud_mlflow Requires: MLFLOW_TRACKING_PASSWORD, MLFLOW_TRACKING_URI in .env
Acceptance: make test-staging has ZERO cloud-related skips.

task.title: Move mamba-ssm reverse skip to conditional marker
task.description:
test_mambavesselnet_construction.py skips "cannot test error path" when mamba-ssm IS installed. This should use a conditional marker that selects the RIGHT test based on whether mamba-ssm is available: - If installed: test the success path - If not installed: test the error path Both paths should be tested, neither should skip.
Acceptance: ZERO skips for mamba-ssm tests in staging.

task.title: Investigate and fix port binding hardening skip
task.description:
test_compose_hardening.py:210 skips with "Port bindings without explicit interface." This is a security test — it should either PASS (ports are properly bound) or FAIL (ports need fixing). Skipping is hiding a security gap.
Investigate: which ports in docker-compose.yml bind to 0.0.0.0 instead of 127.0.0.1? Fix them or document why 0.0.0.0 is intentional.
Acceptance: test either passes or fails. NEVER skips.

task.title: Add -rs flag to make test-staging
task.description:
Add pytest -rs (show skip reasons) to make test-staging so skip reasons are ALWAYS visible in the output. Currently skips are a single number with no context.
Acceptance: make test-staging output shows skip reasons inline.

task.title: Verify: make test-staging has ZERO skips
task.description:
After tasks 1.1-1.3, run make test-staging and verify: - 0 skipped - 0 failed - All cloud tests moved to make test-cloud
Acceptance: "X passed, 0 skipped" in staging output.

phase.name: DeepVess Data Acquisition — Stop Deferring
phase.rationale: The agent has network access, curl, wget, and Python. The agent has GCP credentials via .env. There is NO blocker. The agent must do the work.

task.title: Download DeepVess ZIP from Cornell eCommons
task.description:
Download: https://ecommons.cornell.edu/server/api/core/bitstreams/[UUID]/content Size: 1.45 GB Extract to: data/raw/deepvess/ Organize into: images/ and labels/ subdirectories
The download URL for the ZIP was discovered via DSpace API: https://ecommons.cornell.edu/server/api/core/bundles/fe51aa23-42d6-45a0-b71e-9ce1b00ac63e/bitstreams
Acceptance: data/raw/deepvess/images/ and data/raw/deepvess/labels/ exist with TIFF files.

task.title: DVC track DeepVess
task.description: dvc add data/raw/deepvess git add data/raw/deepvess.dvc .gitignore Acceptance: dvc status shows deepvess tracked.

task.title: DVC push DeepVess to GCS
task.description: dvc push data/raw/deepvess -r gcs Verify: gsutil ls gs://minivess-mlops-dvc-data/files/md5/[hash] Acceptance: round-trip verification succeeds.

phase.name: Cloud Preflight + Dry-Run — Use the Credentials We Have
phase.rationale: .env has GCP credentials. gcloud auth is configured. preflight_gcp.py has 9 checks. The agent must RUN them, not list them as "blocked."

task.title: Check GCP credentials are available
task.description: 1. grep GOOGLE_APPLICATION_CREDENTIALS .env 2. gcloud auth list 3. gsutil ls gs://minivess-mlops-dvc-data/ Report what we have. If missing, tell user EXACTLY what to do. Do NOT just list "GCP credentials" as a blocker.

task.title: Run preflight_gcp.py
task.description: uv run python scripts/preflight_gcp.py Report ALL 9 check results. Fix any failures. Do NOT defer to "next session."

task.title: Run factorial dry-run
task.description: ./scripts/run_factorial.sh --dry-run configs/factorial/debug.yaml Verify: generates correct job configs for 32 + 2 conditions. Report exact output.

task.title: Run make test-cloud
task.description: Run the cloud test tier (created in Phase 1) with actual credentials. Report all results. Fix any failures.

phase.name: Harness Improvement — Enforce Behavior, Don't Document It
phase.rationale: 100 metalearning docs have not changed behavior. The ETH Zurich study (arXiv:2602.11988) shows documentation-based guardrails have hit their ceiling. Enforcement must operate OUTSIDE the LLM reasoning loop.

task.title: Add skip-count enforcement to pre-commit hook
task.description:
Add a pre-commit hook that blocks commits if: 1. The commit message contains "passed" and "skipped" but no skip analysis 2. The staging tier has any skips (after Phase 1 reorganization)
This prevents the agent from committing with unreported skips.

task.title: Add blocker-check to session start
task.description:
When a session starts, the agent should: 1. Check .env for credentials (GCP, HF_TOKEN, MLFLOW) 2. Check data availability (data/raw/deepvess/, data/raw/minivess/) 3. Check Docker image existence 4. Report: "I have access to X, Y, Z" — not "X, Y, Z are blockers"
This prevents the "learned helplessness" pattern where the agent lists available resources as blockers.

task.title: Reduce CLAUDE.md to ~60 lines
task.description:
Per ETH Zurich study and harness improvement plan: move detailed rules to skills (progressive disclosure). Keep only the 10 most critical invariants in CLAUDE.md. This reduces context overhead and may improve rule compliance.
Deferred: requires careful editorial work. Not blocking factorial.

gate.name: launch-ready
gate.description: ALL of the following must be true before launching the factorial: 1. make test-staging: 0 failed, 0 skipped 2. make test-cloud: 0 failed (with actual credentials) 3. preflight_gcp.py: all 9 checks pass 4. DeepVess data on GCS (verified by preflight) 5. Factorial dry-run generates correct 34 job configs 6. User confirms: "Launch for real"
